atoms.semantic_claim: `reth-execution-errors` exposes an `async-context` feature that forwards to `alloy-evm/async-context`, allowing downstream feature chains to enable async-only block execution error variants through this crate.

destinations.evidence: The port crate defines only `default` and `std`; it does not re-export `async-context` at all.
Underlying dependency support still exists in the Ritual alloy-evm dependency, so the missing link is specifically this crate's absent feature forwarding.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: In the port repo, add a minimal feature-resolution check that enables `reth-errors/async-context` or `reth-evm/async-context` from a small crate. It should fail today because `crates/errors/Cargo.toml` and `crates/evm/Cargo.toml` do not define those features, and `crates/evm/execution-errors/Cargo.toml` does not forward `alloy-evm/async-context`.
atoms.required_change: Restore or intentionally replace the `reth-execution-errors` async-context feature re-export so downstream async error-feature propagation can traverse this crate boundary.
atoms.notes: Exact search found this feature only in the downstream source crate. The port crate omits it even though the dependency repo still supports `alloy-evm`'s `async-context` feature.

summary: The downstream-only `async-context` feature re-export on `reth-execution-errors` is absent from the v2.2.0 port, leaving the async error-feature propagation chain unported.
notes: Searches across the port and dependency repos found no exact or near-exact replacement in `reth-execution-errors`. `alloy-evm` still defines `async-context`, but the ported Reth crate family no longer re-exports it here, so this atom remains missing.
